region_of_origin: United States (Mexican-American origin); served in Singapore

ontology.foodex2_description: Corn-based snack with cheese topping — closest parent to cheese loaded nachos composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated 'cheese loaded nachos' composite entry; A0CKP (corn-based snack with cheese) used as closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for cheese loaded nachos; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: gluten (corn tortilla chips may contain trace gluten depending on brand)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of cheese loaded nachos weighs approximately 400 g, providing around 1200 kcal, 80 g fat, and 93 g carbohydrate per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Nachos, cheese (SR Legacy ID 173967)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exactly matches 'cheese loaded nachos' as a restaurant composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for nachos with cheese (FDC ID 173967). Input per_100g values (energy 300 kcal, protein 10 g, fat 20 g, carbohydrate 23.33 g) are consistent with the USDA SR Legacy reference range for this food category and are accepted at medium confidence. The original input provenance was ai_generated from GrabFood (a forbidden primary source); GrabFood values have been cross-validated against the USDA SR Legacy reference and retained only where consistent.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cheese loaded nachos as a composite dish. GI of 63 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for corn tortilla chips (GI ~63, medium category), which constitutes the dominant carbohydrate vehicle in this dish. Cheese, salsa, and jalapeño toppings contribute negligible additional carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × net carbohydrate g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 87.72) / 100 ≈ 55.3, where net carbohydrate = (23.33 - 2.0) g/100g × 400 g serving = 85.32 g; total carbohydrate basis GL = (63 × 93.32) / 100 ≈ 58.8; reported as 55.3 on net carbohydrate basis.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 366.67 mg/100g and saturated fat 10 g/100g; a 400 g serving delivers approximately 1467 mg sodium (64% of the 2300 mg daily limit) and 40 g saturated fat (200% of the 20 g daily limit), both of which elevate blood pressure and promote arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cheese loaded nachos; if consumed, restrict to a 100–150 g portion and request reduced cheese to lower both sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Corn tortilla chips carry a medium-high GI (~63) and a 400 g serving delivers approximately 93 g total carbohydrate with only 8 g dietary fibre, producing a high glycemic load (~55) that drives rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid large portions of cheese loaded nachos; if consumed, limit to 100–150 g, pair with a protein-rich side to blunt glucose response, and monitor blood glucose 1–2 hours post-meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10 g/100g and trans fat at 0.33 g/100g from processed cheese and fried chips raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis; a 400 g serving delivers 40 g saturated fat and 1.32 g trans fat, both well above recommended daily limits.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid cheese loaded nachos due to the combined burden of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium; if consumed on rare occasions, limit to a 100 g portion and choose baked chip variants with reduced-fat cheese.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 10 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold and promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity; nightshade ingredients (jalapeños, tomato-based salsa) may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise cheese loaded nachos; reduce cheese and sour cream to lower saturated fat, monitor for joint flare from nightshade ingredients, and retain guacamole as an anti-inflammatory MUFA-rich element.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheese Loaded Nachos is a Mexican-American snack dish consisting of crispy corn tortilla chips generously topped with melted processed cheese, zesty tomato-based salsa, pickled jalapeños, and optional garnishes such as sour cream and guacamole. The dish is typically assembled and then briefly baked or heated to melt the cheese over the chips, resulting in a combination of crunchy, creamy, spicy, and tangy textures and flavours. It is widely served as a sharing appetiser or party snack in casual dining restaurants, sports bars, and food delivery platforms across Singapore and globally. Known in Chinese as 芝士满载玉米片, in Malay as Nachos Bermuatan Keju, in Tamil as சீஸ் நிறைந்த நாச்சோஸ், and in Hindi as चीज़ लोडेड नाचोज़. Nutritionally, cheese loaded nachos are energy-dense at approximately 300 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 20 g/100g (of which 10 g is saturated fat) and moderate carbohydrate at 23.33 g/100g. The sodium content of 366.67 mg/100g is borderline high, and a standard 400 g plate delivers approximately 1467 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 63 (medium) based on the corn tortilla chip base, with a high glycemic load per serving (~55) due to the large portion size. Clinically, this dish is of high concern for individuals with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and high glycemic load. The dish is suitable for vegetarians but is not vegan, halal-certified, or gluten-free by default.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cheese nachos?
Is nacho cheese high in sodium?
What is the GI of tortilla chips?
Are loaded nachos suitable for hypertension?
How much saturated fat is in cheese nachos?
Can I eat nachos if I have heart disease?

confidence_reason: Input record is ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from GrabFood, a forbidden primary nutrient source. Per_100g values have been cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for nachos with cheese (FDC ID 173967) and found to be within plausible range for this food category. Medium confidence is assigned because no exact approved-source match exists for this specific composite restaurant dish; values are adapted from the closest USDA analogue.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific cheese loaded nachos composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173967) or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed trace/negligible per USDA FoodData Central reference for nachos with cheese; salsa and jalapeños may contribute minimal vitamin C but the composite value rounds to 0.0 at the reported confidence level. This dish is listed as vegetarian in the input dietary_tags; accepted as the described ingredients (chips, cheese, salsa, jalapeños) contain no meat, though halal and kosher status cannot be confirmed without supplier certification. The input ethnic_tags included 'Naan' and 'Burrito' which are not relevant to nachos and have been removed. Two portions were listed in the input with both marked is_default=true; the 400 g plate is used as the canonical default serving for GL calculation. GI of 63 is an analogous estimate based on corn tortilla chips from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for cheese loaded nachos as a composite dish was found in approved sources. Multilingual local names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) added because country_of_origin is Singapore per the input record, triggering Singaporean multilingual naming requirements under G6.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Cheese Loaded Nachos (G00012052) — iteration 3. All six hard gates now pass. G6 resolved by adding mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) triggered by country_of_origin=Singapore. Q8 resolved by adding all local-language strings to both aliases_by_language and flat aliases array. Score 8/8. Final decision: PASS.
